days.label: Push
days.title: Push — Chest & Triceps
days.subtitle: Flat and incline pressing. Cable flys for isolation. Tricep finishers.

exercises.name: Flat Barbell Bench Press
exercises.tip: Eyes under bar, slight arch, shoulder blades pinched. Forearms vertical at the bottom. Elbows 45-75° from torso — not flared to 90°. Half-second pause at chest eliminates momentum and increases chest stimulus.
formTips: Retract and depress shoulder blades before unracking
Drive feet into floor for leg drive
Bar path: slight diagonal from chest to over shoulders

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.tip: Set bench to 30° (one notch up on most benches). Lower dumbbells as deep as your mobility allows — the stretch at the bottom is the primary growth driver with dumbbells vs barbell. Press up and slightly inward.

exercises.name: Low-to-High Cable Fly
exercises.tip: Pulleys at lowest position. Sweep arms up in an arc to chin height. Cables provide constant tension through the entire range — squeeze hard at peak contraction. Slight forward lean.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.tip: Slight 5-10° forward lean to better isolate the lateral (side) delt. Stop at shoulder height — going higher recruits traps. Imagine pouring water from a pitcher. Strict form always beats heavy weight on this exercise.

exercises.name: Cable Fly
exercises.tip: Set pulleys at chest height. Lock your elbow angle throughout (slight bend). Try crossing hands slightly at peak for extra inner chest activation. Control the eccentric — don't let the cables yank your arms back.

exercises.name: Rope Tricep Pulldown
exercises.tip: Split the rope apart at the bottom and hold for a beat — this targets the lateral head which gives arm width (the horseshoe shape). Lock elbows at your sides throughout. If your elbows drift forward, the weight is too heavy.

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.tip: The long head of the triceps only fully stretches when the arm is overhead — pulldowns alone won't fully develop your triceps. Get a deep stretch behind your head every rep. Use cable or dumbbell.

days.label: Pull
days.title: Pull — Back & Biceps
days.subtitle: Rowing and pulldown variations. Pull-ups. Triple bicep finisher.

exercises.name: Low Machine Row
exercises.tip: Pull with your elbows, not your hands — imagine hands are hooks. Let the weight stretch you forward slightly at the bottom (protract shoulders). Then squeeze shoulder blades together hard for a full second at peak contraction. This builds back thickness.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Full stretch at top; lean back 15°
exercises.tip: Lean back 15-20°. Let your shoulders fully elevate at the top of each rep — the stretch is the most important part for lat growth. Many people cut this short and miss the best part. Pull to upper chest, not behind neck.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.tip: Pull rope to face height, then split the rope and rotate knuckles toward the ceiling. The external rotation at the end is what makes this exercise special — it targets rear delts and rotator cuff muscles critical for shoulder health and the 3D shoulder look.

exercises.name: Pull-Ups
exercises.tip: Start from a full dead hang every rep (arms fully extended, shoulders elevated). This full range of motion is critical — partial pull-ups miss the stretched position where most growth happens. If you can't get 6 reps, use a band for assistance. Add weight with a belt once you hit 3×10.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.notes: Long head stretch; arms behind torso
exercises.tip: Set bench to 45-60°. Let arms hang behind your torso — keep elbows pinned behind your body throughout. This is one of the only exercises that targets the bicep long head (the peak) in its stretched position. Slow 3-second lowering.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.tip: The preacher pad eliminates momentum and cheating. Lower all the way down to full extension — don't cut the bottom short. The short head of the bicep gets maximum stimulus at the bottom of this movement. Control the negative.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.tip: Keep a neutral grip throughout (thumbs pointing up). This targets the brachialis, which sits under the biceps and pushes it up when developed — it adds arm width visible from the front. Don't swing.

days.label: Legs
days.title: Legs — Quads, Hamstrings & Glutes
days.subtitle: Squat pattern. Unilateral work. Machine isolation. Hip thrust for glutes.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.tip: Hip crease below knee every rep. Big belly breath into your belt (or brace hard), push knees out over toes. Full-depth squats recruit significantly more muscle fiber than partials. Film yourself from the side to check depth until it's automatic.
formTips: Brace core before descent — belly breath into belt
Knees track over toes, push out slightly

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.notes: DB in each hand; front shin vertical
exercises.tip: Step far enough forward that your front shin is vertical at the bottom. Rear foot is for balance only — don't push off it. Upright torso = more quad emphasis. Slight forward lean = more glute. This builds single-leg strength and fixes imbalances.

exercises.name: Hamstring Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Slow 3 sec eccentric; point toes away
exercises.tip: Point toes away from you (plantarflexion) to reduce calf involvement and isolate hamstrings. The 3-second lowering phase is where the magic happens — hamstrings respond exceptionally well to slow eccentrics. Curl as close to your glutes as possible.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Full lockout; squeeze 1 sec at top
exercises.tip: Full lockout at the top, squeeze your quads hard for 1 second every rep — the peak contraction is uniquely valuable on this exercise. Align your knees with the machine's pivot point. Don't let the weight stack slam between reps.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust Machine
exercises.notes: Full lockout; squeeze glutes at top
exercises.tip: Drive through your heels. At the top, squeeze your glutes hard and hold for a beat — full hip extension is where glute activation peaks. Don't hyperextend your lower back; think about tucking your pelvis slightly at lockout. Posterior pelvic tilt at top.

days.label: Shldr/Abs
days.title: Shoulders & Abs
days.subtitle: Overhead pressing. Lateral delts. Core-focused ab work.

exercises.name: Shoulder Dumbbell Press
exercises.tip: Seated with bench at 80-85° (not fully vertical). Lower dumbbells to ear level minimum every rep — the stretched position at the bottom is where growth stimulus is highest. Press up and slightly inward. Don't bounce at the bottom.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Lead with pinkies; second session for volume
exercises.tip: This is your second lateral raise session of the week — side delts recover fast and respond well to high frequency. Same cues: slight lean, stop at shoulder height, lead with pinkies. Consider doing a drop set on the last set for extra stimulus.

exercises.name: Dragon Flag
exercises.notes: Advanced; regress to negatives if needed
exercises.tip: Grip bench behind your head. Body stays rigid as a plank — lower your entire body as one unit. If you can't do full reps yet, do slow negatives only (lower over 5 seconds, reset at top). This is an advanced movement; there's no shame in partial range of motion while building strength.

exercises.name: Weighted Leg Raise
exercises.notes: Curl pelvis up; ankle weight or DB
exercises.tip: The key is pelvic tilt — curl your belt buckle toward your chin at the top. Without this, hip flexors do 80% of the work and abs barely engage. Add 2-5 lb ankle weights rather than doing higher reps; resistance drives ab hypertrophy more effectively than volume.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Exhale hard at bottom; hips locked
exercises.tip: Hips stay locked in place — don't sit back onto your heels (that's a hip flexion, not a crunch). Forceful exhale at the bottom activates the transverse abdominis and increases contraction intensity. Think ribcage moving toward pelvis, not arms pulling the rope.
